channel.title: Comments for page "The Happy Ending"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Happy Ending"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quality tale right here. I also love depictions of the Foundation just being so used to 682 they're just Frenemies now (even if they do decide to blow the lizard sky high).

Although, I would like to note that now that SCP-4321 has been posted, and its not a girl with a disease from AWCY? that should probably changed as to avoid confusion.

item.title: Re: Why kill though?
item.content:encoded:
There is an effort necessary to contain him that involves triggering his constant survival/adaptation process to distract him; that's the basis of his current containment, the acid tank. Do you know for sure that this hyperintelligent, hyperaggressive survival machine won't use the entire Earth's ecosystem for resources to escape the planet? 682 is in general too much of a threat to keep contained, we don't have to allow his continued life, and Bright chose not to.

Killing the money eating lady was an act of mercy, because she was suffering as it was. The cockatrice was annoying. (I actually picked the cockatrice using Random SCP; I don't hold anything in particular against it.)

item.title: Re: Why kill though?
item.content:encoded: This

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I'm the reverse

item.title: Why kill though?
item.content:encoded:
If they had tech so advanced that they could keep him trapped for centuries and make him mortal in an instant, how come they didn't had tech advanced enough to make the whole planet his containment enclosure?

Now I'm thinking about it, what made those other two SCPs require termination as well? Surely with future tech they could've made some sort of lab-grown pulsating meat thing to feed the cockatrice lizard thing without having to kill any more bunnies, and some robots to take care of them without any risk to the rest of the living things; and surely they would've been able to find a way to keep the money eating lady young and non-infectious…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounded like an engineered infectious disease that made people compulsively eat money and money-like documents…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What was the 4321 thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Think I found my headcanon.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love what you did to 682, and absolutely hate you for what you did to 682 (character-wise). Congratulations. +1

Now I'm UPSET.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "former containers" ==> "former jailors" . Better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I shed a couple of tears….and then I upvoted in such a blazing glory of furiousness that I might've hurt my upvoting finger.

Edit: However, the phrase "former containers" seems a bit confusing and out of place. Otherwise, I love it. I love the idea of a tired, old Dr. Bright and a tired, old 682 in the future.
And then you broke my heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but feel that nukes wouldn't kill 682. That's the only thing in this story that bothers me. Otherwise, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meshes well with my 682 headcanon: sure, it hates people, but after a few hundred years of nothing much happening and never coming face to face with a human…

Aside from that, I liked it up until the ending, which felt like a bit of a cop-out. Suddenly, 682 dies? Wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't feel like Dr. Bright at all to me, and the concept is extremely 'meh', but I enjoyed the story (minus a few awkward moments) well enough to squint my eyes, accept the personality transplant, and upvote.

EDIT: Also, I love 'polite' 682. Not my headcanon at all, but I love what you did with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations. You just made me feel sorry for 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Straight up.

*fist bumps*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no canon, my son. Vaya con 343.

In other words: Cool beans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, so someone went and developed a source of infinite energy, and then someone else made it so that no one could ever know about it?

That's pretty cool to be honest.

But I like my 055 like I like my Batman: non-overpowered but capable of anything depending on the writer. I'll just agree to disagree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wrote this so long ago that I don't recall how I explained it in the story, but in my head as I was writing it, 055 was always capable of the teleportation and power production thing, it was just designed to conceal it. 682 entering it (my headcanon for 682 being that of a metaphysical glitch, so to speak; a bug in the coding of the universe) damaged some of the anti-meme component of the device. But in general, this isn't designed to influence anybody's headcanon of 055, because I don't fully have one myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay then, but even if it's effect did include mass duplication/transportation, how were we able to remember where all of this new energy was coming from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you prove it's not what 055 does?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So 682 stumbles into 055 and has inferior copies teleported to major cities? I don't understand the logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see 682 being respectful to an inferior creature who has hurt it multiple times. Destroying it messes with my headcanon, but I'm ok with that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or that it died. The zappy-gun, and the nukes, they obliterated it. Sent it back to the space beyond space it came from.

That's where it told the rest of its kin about Earth, and the horrible, *disgusting* things that spawned there and spread among the stars. And they didn't want to take the chance of us ever finding their home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this was a typical 682 story we'd find out that he hadn't died.

Or that she left eggs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's cute, and part of me wishes the SCP universe could hace something like this, but it's too jarringly out-of-genre otherwise. Respectful 682 is bad enOugh, but then you went and applied-phlebotinized it to death… no. Sorry, but no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, think about it this way: could a bear in the woods construct a vat of acid to dunk it in? Would a flock of geese design intricate, modular weapon arrays just to keep it off guard? Is a grove of apple trees going to detonate nuclear warheads? Of course not. With humans (and all other intelligent beings, one would presume) no longer on the planet, 682 could run roughshod and kill, kill, kill, until there's nothing left to kill and Khorne is a happy chaos god.

…. Or at least he would have if he wasn't zapped with magic-future-tech and then nuked into oblivion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why the capital A in animals?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, they probably realize that it's only a matter of time before they become exposed, and they'd rather have things happen this way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Think of it this way: The Foundation has seen no choice but to do horrible things. Now they have a choice. Their mandate hasn't changed, but 682's actions have enabled them to do things differently. And I can also see the Foundation throwing its hands up after the Rampage and deciding that the new world needs a new approach, now that amensiacs are kind of pointless, other organizations have an increase in membership…

If it's a brave new world, then perhaps it requires a brave new Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Trying to reconcile how much this messes with my head canon vs. how much I liked this story.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He probably wouldn't. He'd just stay away from those planets. An entomophobe doesn't try to seek out and destroy all insects, he'd try to avoid them instead. If he had no choice, as 682 does, then he tries to kill them. But the first choice is avoidance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I-I actually shed a tear at this story.

UPVOTED.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was REALLY close to writing that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I wouldn't have used a preexisting character, except I knew I was setting this in the far future, and it would be sort of contrived to create a new immortal Foundation staff member just for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was actually waiting for the part where everyone on Earth is just a couple hundred miles away, hoping that 682 doesn't call their bluff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my head-canon, Jack/Bright uses the Secret Chord at the end, just to be sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I enjoy the tone and style, I can't say I'm too fond of the message. This is the organization that has restructured reality several times. That has contained reality benders and made people accept the concept of colour (SCP-8900-EX). I can't see a few cities being damaged by the eternal antagonist causing them to change their mandate.

The Foundation is more than just a bureaucracy who changes their motive based on public opinion/knowledge. It is not in the business of forgiveness or mercy, for these concepts have no bearing on containment for the sake of the maintaining peace in a war against a hostile universe. It is an extension of human fear, doubt and blind hope made manifest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been growing gradually less fond of stories involving senior staff over time, even the ones meant to deconstruct the earlier ones tend not to work for me. This one wasn't like that. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. Another one that just doesn't work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i really liked it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right, that's why Bright kills it. He doesn't actually trust it. As for the respect thing, some of that has to do with my headcanon regarding 682, so I understand if it's not for everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice use of SCP-055 and "Are We Cool Yet?". But I'm not sold on 682 being respectful in any possible way. Besides, he doesn't just hate humans. He hates all earth life, including plants and Animals. And if he was told there were humans on other planets he'd find a way to get them too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great story. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, and credit to Clef for the first line, lifted from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite entertaining, even if I might have a hard time keeping it the fuck away from my headcanon. Good work, Eskobar.
